Explore the causes

Check each component

  • Use exact method and update demand shocks.

m_f + m_w = 1

  • Pick up normal ths in normal markets (i.e., \(c_i^W > 0\) and \(c_i^F > 0\) for all \(i\) in the market)
    • t = 1, j = 1
  • Make two graphs:
    • Change \(\lambda_w\)
      • In the y-axis of the same graph, overlay the lines of
      • \(c_i^W\)
      • \(- \frac{s_i^W(\boldsymbol w, \boldsymbol f)}{\frac{\partial s_i^W}{\partial w_i}}\)
      • \(f_i - w_i\).
    • Change \(\lambda_f\)
      • \(c_i^F\)
      • term 1: \(\frac{q_i(\boldsymbol f) s_i^W(\boldsymbol w, \boldsymbol f)}{S^F \frac{\partial s_i^F (\boldsymbol f )}{\partial f_i}}\)
      • term 2: \(\frac{q_i(\boldsymbol f) \frac{\partial s_i^W(\boldsymbol w, \boldsymbol f)}{\partial f_i}}{S^F \frac{\partial s_i^F (\boldsymbol f )}{\partial f_i}}(f_i - w_i - c_i^W)\)
      • term 3:\(\frac{\frac{\partial q_i(\boldsymbol f)}{\partial f_i}}{S^F \frac{\partial s_i^F (\boldsymbol f )}{\partial f_i}} s_i^W(\boldsymbol w, \boldsymbol f) (f_i - w_i - c_i^W)\)

  • Pick up a problematic ths in a problematic market (i.e., \(c_i^W < 0\) or \(c_i^F < 0\) for \(i\) in the market)
    • t = 1, j = 7, i = 2

lambda_w

Why \(\frac{\partial s_i^W}{\partial w_i}\) is not monotone in \(\lambda_w\)?

  • From the above graph, pick up the point before and after the jump in \(\frac{\partial s_i^W}{\partial w_i}\).
  • Check why this is so different.
